gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/350)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/350)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) كَانَتْ لِي جَارِيَةٌ حُبْلَى فَنَذَرْتُ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنْ وَلَدَتْ غُلَاماً أَنْ أُحِجَّهُ أَوْ أَحُجَّ عَنْهُ فَقَالَ إِنَّ رَجُلًا نَذَرَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي ابْنٍ لَهُ إِنْ هُوَ أَدْرَكَ أَنْ يَحُجَّ عَنْهُ أَوْ يُحِجَّهُ فَمَاتَ الْأَبُ وَ أَدْرَكَ الْغُلَامُ بَعْدُ فَأَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْغُلَامُ فَسَأَلَهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَأَمَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَنْ يُحَجَّ عَنْهُ مِمَّا تَرَكَ أَبُوهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Raib, from Misma’a who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘There was a slave girl of mine who was pregnant. So I vowed for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic that if she were to give birth to a boy, I would send him for Hajj, or I would perform Hajj on his behalf’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘A man vowed for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic regarding a son of his, that when he is mature he would either perform Hajj of his behalf, or send him to Hajj. So the father dies and the boy matured afterwards. So the boy came to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, to ask him<sup>saww</sup> about that. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> ordered him that he should perform Hajj on his behalf, from what his father had neglected’.
en.sarwar: 25. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from Misma‘ who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of my girl who was pregnant and I made a vow and commitment for Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, that if she gives birth to a boy, I will help him to perform al-Hajj or I will perform al-Hajj on his behalf. He (the Imam) said, ‘A man made a vow and commitment for Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, about his son that if he matures he will perform al-Hajj for him or help him to perform al-Hajj. The father died and the boy became mature thereafter and came to the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, who commanded him to perform al-Hajj by means of what his father has left behind.’”
